runtime.version: current vLLM realtime endpoint at the evaluated model revision
runtime.boundary: official Python/CUDA serving path with a single GPU requiring at least 16 GB

options.llama_cpp_assumption: none; current official vLLM realtime support was recorded instead

energy.notes: No accepted Apple-local runtime reached inference.

engineering_cost.conversion_effort: An owned path needs the causal audio encoder, adapter, sliding-window decoder, realtime framing, token feedback, and quantized weight conversion.
engineering_cost.update_cost: The available community MLX runtime would need to be replaced by owned task code and tracked against the official architecture.
engineering_cost.packaging_cost: The official repository stores about 17.77 GB and requires at least 16 GB GPU memory in its recommended BF16 serving path.

reproduction.command: No product benchmark command: the official realtime runtime is vLLM/CUDA and the community MLX path is reference-only.

result.blocker: The Apache-2.0 model has a current official vLLM realtime implementation with configurable delay, but not an accepted native Apple runtime. Its community MLX conversion demonstrates feasibility only; adopting it would bypass ADR-0005, while an owned 4B port is a larger first step than the measured Qwen3-ASR 0.6B candidate.
observations: The current official model card, rather than a stale llama.cpp status, defines the evaluated vLLM runtime.
Its native streaming architecture remains attractive after the smaller Qwen adapter proves maintainable.
The model is future research, not a reason to block the selected Apple-primary product path.
